name: Audio Planning
summary: Plan audio assets for narrative scope
purpose: Plan audio content for a narrative scope. Create Audio Plans for all worthy moments (planning is cheap), organize into Cuelists with priorities, maintain Reference Sheets for sonic consistency. Generation is separate and budget-constrained.
description: Creates audio_plan artifacts for audio content. Audio Director identifies sound opportunities (ambient/spot/music/voice), assigns priorities (must_have/nice_to_have/optional), and maintains reference_sheet artifacts for sonic consistency. Style Lead validates audio aesthetic alignment. Does NOT generate audio - plans are ready for generate_audio tool when budget allows.

triggers.condition: New chapter, act, or book scope ready for audio planning
Sections from Scene Weave approved and stable
Reference Sheet needed for recurring sonic element (leitmotif, location ambient)

optional_artifacts.description: Approved sections to plan audio for
Unified style guide for audio aesthetic

context_requirements: Style Guide from workspace (audio_aesthetic: sonic signature, ambient style, music guidance)
Existing Reference Sheets for recurring sonic elements
Current Cuelist if updating an existing scope
Generation budget if moving to render phase

optional_artifacts.description: Organized index of Audio Plans with priorities
New or updated Reference Sheets for recurring sonic elements

audio_plan_creation.name: Audio Plan Creation
audio_plan_creation.purpose: Identify audio opportunities and create Audio Plans for all worthy moments

survey_scope.action: Review sections to identify audio opportunities. Consider: atmosphere-setting moments, tension-building scenes, important choices, recurring locations, emotional beats.
survey_scope.guidance: Think like a player: where would sound help them feel immersed, remember something, or notice a cue? Plan liberally—we can prioritize later. Look for: atmosphere (set mood), signpost (signal transition or choice), recall (memory anchor), clarify (make things tangible).

outputs.name: opportunity_list
outputs.description: List of moments/scenes worth audio enhancement with rationale

create_audio_plans.action: Create Audio Plan for each identified opportunity. Specify cue type, description, purpose, priority, and accessibility transcript.
create_audio_plans.guidance: Every Audio Plan must have a clear purpose (atmosphere/signpost/recall/clarify) and cue type (ambient/spot/music/voice). Set realistic priorities: must_have for critical moments, nice_to_have for enhancing moments, optional for nice touches. Write accessible transcripts.

identify_reference_needs.action: Identify recurring sonic elements that need Reference Sheets for consistency.
identify_reference_needs.guidance: Sonic elements appearing in 2+ cues need consistency tracking: location ambients (recurring settings like 'the office'), character leitmotifs (if character has a musical theme), significant objects with sound signatures. These can use subject_type 'location' or 'object' Reference Sheets with audio notes.

outputs.name: reference_needs
outputs.description: List of recurring sonic elements requiring Reference Sheets

completion_criteria: All worthy moments have Audio Plans
Each Audio Plan has cue type, purpose, priority, and transcript
Recurring sonic elements needing sheets are identified

reference_sheet_maintenance.name: Reference Sheet Maintenance
reference_sheet_maintenance.purpose: Create or update Reference Sheets for recurring sonic elements to ensure audio consistency

create_sheets.action: Create Reference Sheets for sonic elements that need them. For locations: characteristic ambient sounds, atmosphere. For objects: sound signatures. For characters with leitmotifs: musical theme description.
create_sheets.guidance: Audio consistency matters less per-cue than visual (sounds don't need to match as precisely as faces), but key sonic signatures should be documented. Use subject_type 'location' or 'object' with audio-focused descriptions. Leitmotifs are rare—only for significant characters.

link_sheets_to_plans.action: Update Audio Plans with reference_refs pointing to relevant Reference Sheets.
link_sheets_to_plans.guidance: Every Audio Plan featuring a sheeted sonic element should reference that sheet. This ensures consistency across related cues (all 'office' ambients sound similar).

outputs.name: linked_audio_plans
outputs.description: Audio Plans with reference sheet links

completion_criteria: All identified recurring sonic elements have Reference Sheets

cuelist_organization.name: Cuelist Organization
cuelist_organization.purpose: Organize Audio Plans into a Cuelist with summary statistics and budget tracking

build_cuelist.action: Create or update Cuelist for this scope. Organize Audio Plans, compute statistics by cue type/purpose/priority/status.
build_cuelist.guidance: The Cuelist is the planning manifest. It shows what's planned, what's prioritized, and what budget remains. Use this to communicate with Showrunner about generation priorities.

completion_criteria: Cuelist contains all Audio Plans for scope
Statistics computed for cue type, purpose, priority, and status

audio_aesthetic_review.name: Audio Aesthetic Review
audio_aesthetic_review.purpose: Style Lead reviews Audio Plans for audio aesthetic alignment before generation

check_audio_alignment.action: Review Audio Plans for alignment with established audio aesthetic: sonic signature, ambient style, musical guidance.
check_audio_alignment.guidance: Cross-reference Audio Plans against Style Guide audio_aesthetic section. Flag drift but don't block Audio Director decisions on WHAT to sound—only flag HOW concerns (descriptions that violate sonic signature, cue types that clash with established aesthetic).

apply_audio_feedback.action: Apply Style Lead feedback to Audio Plans where warranted.
apply_audio_feedback.guidance: Audio Director has final say on WHAT and WHERE. Accept HOW feedback (sonic descriptions, layering guidance) that aligns with Style Guide.

outputs.name: refined_audio_plans
outputs.description: Audio Plans with audio aesthetic alignment

completion_criteria: All Audio Plans reviewed for audio aesthetic

on_failure.message: Significant audio aesthetic issues. Revise Audio Plans with Style Lead guidance.

ready_for_generation.name: Ready for Generation
ready_for_generation.purpose: Mark Audio Plans as ready and await budget allocation for generation

finalize_plans.action: Transition Audio Plans from draft to ready state. Update Cuelist statistics.
finalize_plans.guidance: Plans are now ready for generation when budget allows. The generate_audio tool can be called for any ready plan. Priority order: must_have first, then nice_to_have, then optional.

completion_criteria: All reviewed Audio Plans are in ready state
Generation can proceed when budget is allocated

on_success.message: Audio Plans ready for generation. Call generate_audio tool for individual plans as budget allows. Update Cuelist as audio is rendered.
